sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Establish a one-year independent Cardano dOSPO and Open Maintenance Framework program to manage open source sustainability funds, create advisory councils, form a 501(c)(3) public charity, fund maintenance retainers and related programs, and publish quarterly reports.
Recipient: Christian Taylor / Open Source Cowboy Consulting as administrator at treasury withdrawal, with intended operational transition to a dOSPO legal entity subject to governance.

Deliverables: dOSPO operational team and program management systems, two advisory councils with published feedback before disbursements, 501(c)(3) dOSPO legal entity and published governance documents, quarterly public reports with Mill Law Firm financial review, dependency audit with SBOMs, centrality scores, bus-factor assessments, and coverage gap analysis, maintenance fund retainers for high-risk Cardano open source infrastructure, public CHAOSS-aligned dashboard, end-of-term portfolio review
Deadline/expiry: 12 months, with stated milestones at weeks 6, 12, 13, 16, 18, 24, 26, 39, and 52
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 4,094,000 ADA over 12 months for a dOSPO and OMF program.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Christian Taylor / Open Source Cowboy Consulting is designated as the sole Article II.7.5 administrator from treasury withdrawal through the 12-month period.
Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Mill Law Firm is designated as independent financial auditor on a quarterly cadence through Month 12.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal states that no WP2-WP5 disbursement will be made before advisory council feedback is published, while final allocation authority remains with the administrator.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The program will form two advisory councils by Week 6 and a 501(c)(3) dOSPO legal entity by Week 24, subject to legal processes and governance.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): WP2 allocates 2,000,000 ADA to maintenance retainers for Cardano open source infrastructure selected using dependency centrality, bus-factor analysis, SBOMs, and a published rubric.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal asserts that Cardano lacks an operational layer to translate governance decisions into sustained accountable maintenance programs.
Independent evidence for: The proposal claims the dOSPO and OMF whitepapers were developed with experts from several named organizations and communities.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 4,094,000 ADA over 12 months for a dOSPO and OMF program." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal asserts that Cardano lacks an operational layer to translate governance decisions into sustained accountable maintenance programs." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
